features.summary: Tesla reported a record Q2 2021 with breakthrough production, deliveries, and over $1 billion in GAAP net income for the first time. Auto gross margins excluding credits increased substantially due to cost optimization, production scale, and some pricing actions. The company is managing through global chip shortages that remain the primary constraint on production growth, requiring significant engineering efforts to substitute alternative chips and rewrite firmware. New Gigafactories in Texas and Berlin are progressing toward limited Model Y production later in 2021, featuring structural battery packs and 4680 cells.
features.management_outlook: Management expects volume growth to be determined by supply chain constraints rather than demand or factory capacity, with chip shortages remaining the primary bottleneck. Tesla aims for at least 50% growth next year, potentially reaching 100% if supply chain issues resolve. The company is targeting roughly double cell supply from suppliers in 2022 and expects to reach 100 GWh annual production rate from internal 4680 cell manufacturing sometime next year. FSD subscription launched but is expected to gain momentum primarily next year when Full Self-Driving capabilities are more widely available.
features.risks: Global semiconductor shortages continue to severely constrain production, with Tesla having no control over resolution timing. Manufacturing ramp complexity remains extreme, with production moving only as fast as the slowest of roughly 10,000 unique parts and processes. New 4680 battery cell production faces engineering challenges in scaling from pilot to high-volume manufacturing, particularly with calendaring equipment. Cybertruck and Semi production timelines depend on resolving chip shortages and scaling cell capacity, with both vehicles requiring significant cell supply that could impact other product lines.
features.key_drivers: Production scaling success depends on resolving semiconductor supply constraints and localizing supply chains for new factories. 4680 battery cell technology represents a critical cost and performance advantage once manufacturing challenges are resolved. Supercharger network expansion to other EVs could improve utilization and profitability while supporting sustainable transport adoption. Energy storage demand significantly exceeds supply capacity, with Megapack sold out through end of next year and massive Powerwall backlog representing substantial growth opportunity once cell and chip supply constraints are alleviated.
